raw_text: ABBOTT, A. W., C.B.E. (1949).—b. 1893; ed. Blundell's; entd. C.A. off. after compet. exam., 1912; mil. serv., 1917-19; lieut.; asst. hd., fin. dept., 1929; dep. hd., 1933; hd., 1938; sec., E.A. currency bd., 1930-38; dep. ch. clk., 1945; ch. clk. (estab. offr.), 1948.

ABBOTT, C. T.—b. 1897; ed. Portora Royal Sch., Enniskillen, N. Ireland, and Hon. Soc'y of Gray's Inn; barrister-at-law; mil. serv., 1914-19, lieut.; res. mag., Ken., 1930; crown coun., 1930; pres., dist. ct., Cyp., 1932; solr.-gen., S.L., 1938; puisne judge, Nig., 1944; Mal., 1950.

ABBOTT, M. J.—b. 1906; ed. King's Sch., Canterbury; barrister. Gray's Inn; mil. serv., 1941-45, lieut.; asst. crown solr., H.K., 1936; seconded to Ethiopian Govt. as pres., high ct., 1946; puisne judge, Nig., 1950.

ABBOTT, T. K.—b. 1912; ed. King's Sch., Parramatta, Univ. Sydney and Sydney Hosp.; mil. serv., 1941-45, maj.; M.O.H., H.K., 1940; resig. and re-entered Borneo, 1945; ag. D.M. and H.S., Sarawak, 1945; M.O.H., Tang., 1947; S.M.O., Sey., 1948; D.D.M.S., N. Borneo, 1951.
ABELL, Sir Anthony, K.C.M.G. (1952), C.M.G.—b. 1906; ed. Repton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; cadet, Nig., 1929; admin. offr. cl. II, 1946; cl. I, 1948; gov. and c.in-c., Sarawak, 1950.

ABERNETHY, J. S.—b. 1907; ed. Sedbergh Sch., Aberdeen Gram. Sch., and Aberdeen and Edin. Univs.; chartered co., N. Borneo; legal advr., comsnr. of lands, prot. of lab. (incl. inspr., schs.), 1936; food contrlr. (in addn.), 1941; sessions judge, B.M.A. (B. Borneo), 1946; N. Borneo Col.; comsnr., lands, 1947; sessions judge, 1948; res. mag., Tang., 1949; puisne judge, 1951.
ABRAHAM, A. L.—b. 1908; ed. Southend High Sch.; police, Pal., 1930; supt., Barb., 1938; Fiji, 1944; B. Hond., 1950.

ABRAHAMS, Rt. Hon. Sir Sidney, P.C. (1941), Kt. Bach. (1936), Q.C.—b. 1885; ed. Bedford Modern Sch. and Emmanuel Coll., Camb.; called to Bar, Middle Temp., June, 1909; hon. Bencher, 1948; Midland circuit; town mag., Zanz., 1915; cr. coun., Ken., 1915 (did not proceed); seconded for serv. with Mespot. civ. admin., 1920; advoc.-gen. and registr. of companies, Baghdad, 1920; pres., ct. of first instance, ct. of sessions and ct. of criminal appl., Basrah, 1921; atty.-gen., Zanz., 1922; Uga., 1925; G.C., 1928; ch. just., Uga., 1933; Tang., 1934; Ceylon, 1936-39; member of jud. comttee. of privy cl., 1941; jt. edr., Zanz. law rept., 1868-1918; sole edr., 1918-22; 2nd cls. Order of Brilliant Star of Zanz., 1924; addl. asst. leg. advr., C.O., 1939; sole comsnr. to enquire into Nyasaland land problems, 1946; edited (with A. Abrahams) Dagens Nyheter Stadian Edn., 1912; various contributions to journalism on track athletics.
ACKLAND, R. B.—b. 1898; ed. Suva Gram. Sch.; clk., Fiji, 1917; asst. audr., 1939; asst. comsnr., inl. rev., 1946; comsnr., 1950.

ACOLATSE, C. S.—b. 1899; ed. Livingstone Coll., Salisbury, N.C., U.S.A., and Cambridge Univ.; barrister-at-law (Lincoln's Inn), 1930; in practice, Gold Coast, 1931-43; dist. mag., 1943; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1952.

ADAMS, C. O.—b. 1907; ed. Forensic Sci. Coll., Nottingham, and Met. Police Detective Trng. Coll., Hendon; mil. serv., 1939-42, capt.; police cadet, J'ca, 1930; asst. supt., 1932; asst. comsnr., police, Nig., 1935; supt., Trin., 1939; sp. br., Scotland Yd., 1949; supt., constab., N. Borneo, 1949; dep. comsnr., Sarawak constab., 1951.
ADAMS, H. L.—b. 1910; ed. Haileybury; mil. serv., 1939-45; admin. cadet, Ken., 1946; sec., comrce. and industry, 1949.

ADDIS, W., C.M.G. (1948), Brill. Star (3rd cl.), Zanz. (1946).—b. 1901; ed. Rugby and Magdalene Coll., Camb.; war serv., 1939-45, lieut., Zanz. N.V.F.; asst. dist. comsnr., Zanz., 1924; seconded to D.O., 1933; dist. comsnr., Zanz., 1933; dist. offr., N. Rhod., 1936; dist. comsnr., Zanz., 1938; col. sec., Berm., 1945; ag. gov., Oct., 1945-May, 1946, and Mar.-Oct., 1949; dep. comsnr.-gen. for col. affrs., S.E. Asia, 1950.

ADDISON, W. R. L., M.C.—b. 1919; ed. Haileybury Coll. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1939-46, maj.; cadet, Uganda, 1948; A.D.O., 1950; asst. sec., E.A.H.C., 1952; clk., cent. leg. assembly, 1951.

ADEMOLA, A. A.—b. 1906; ed. King's Coll., Lagos, and Camb. Univ.; barrister-at-law; mag., Nig., 1939; puisne judge, 1949; mem. of comsns. for revision of cts. legn. (1948) and to enq. into Enugu disturbances (1949), Nig.

ADIE, J. J.—b. 1913; ed. Kingsland Grange Prep. Sch., Shrewsbury Sch. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1940-42 (K.A.R. and O.E.T.A.); admin. offr., Zanz., 1937; asst. dist. comsnr., Pemba, 1938; Zanz., 1939; p.s. to Br. Res., 1939-40; asst. sec., 1942; senr. asst. sec. and p.s. to H.H. the Sultan, 1946; awarded Brill. Star of Zanz., (4th cl.), 1947; seconded C.O., 1949; sec., lab. and educ., Ken., 1952; author of various articles on Zanzibar.

ADU, A. L., O.B.E. (1953).—b. 1914 ; ed. Presb. Church Schls., Boso and Anum, Achimota Coll. and Sch., and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; Master, Achimota Coll. and Sch., 1939 ; asst. dist. comsnr., 1942 ; comsnr. for Africanisation, 1950.
AGOSTINI, C.—b. 1914 ; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trin.; control offr., Trin., 1941 ; dep. aerodrome supt., 1946 ; dir. of civil aviation, 1947.

AIKMAN, R. G., C.M.G.—b. 1905 ; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Bristol Univ.; admin. cadet, Sarawak, 1926 ; dist. offr., 1936 ; resdt., 1940 ; civ. intern., 1941-45 ; dep. ch. sec., 1947 ; ch. sec., 1950 ; O.A.G., 1952.
AINLEY, A. J., M.C.—b. 1906 ; ed. St. Bees Sch. and Oxford Univ.; barrister-at-law; mil. serv., 1939-45, maj.; dist. mag., G.C., 1935 ; cr. coun., 1936 ; judge, Uga., 1946.
AITKEN, I. H.—b. 1919 ; ed. Greenock High Sch. and St. Andrews Univ.; tax assessor, Ken., 1944 ; senr. immigr. offr., 1948 ; dep. prin. immig. offr., 1952.

AITKEN, Maj. R.—b. 1910 ; ed. Gregg Comm. Coll. Ipswich; mil. serv., 1939-46, major; H.M. Prison Service, 1936 ; second. supt., prisons, B. Hond., 1946 ; resigned, joined N.Z. Prisons Serv., 1947 ; re-appointed supt., prisons, B. Hond., 1949 ; dep. dir. of prisons, J'ca, 1951.
ALBURY, K. H. E.—b. 1920 ; ed. Govt. High Sch., Nassau; clk., public treas., Bah., 1940 ; asst. audr., 1950.

ALCOCK, A. E. S.—b. 1902 ; ed. County High Sch., Ilford, and Lond. Univ.; apptd. Ceylon, 1928 ; Tang. 1930 ; town engrnr., Kumasi, G.C., 1936 ; sec. and exec. offr., town and country planning bd., 1945.

ALCOCK, W. G.—b. 1907 ; ed. Oxford Prep. Sch., Glasgow Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; solr. (Scotland); asst. lands offr., Tang., 1935 ; lands offr. and registr.-gen., Nyasa., 1939 ; official assignee, S'pore, 1948 ; comsnr. of estate duties and stamps in addition.
ALEMA, G. N.—b. 1902 ; ed. Sierra Leone Gram. Sch., Fourah Bay Coll. and Univ. of Oxford; dep. registr., co-op. societies (consumer development), G.C., 1948.

ALEXANDER, W. G., M.B.E.—b. 1918 ; ed. Sedbergh Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1940-45, major; admin. cadet, G. & E. Is. Col., 1946 ; co-op. soc. offr., 1947 ; registr., co-op. societies, Maur., 1951 ; survey and Report on possibilities co-op. dev. and necessary co-op. legislation in G. & E. Is., 1947.
ALEXIS, F. C.—b. 1913 ; ed. Grenada Boys Secondary Sch. and Royal Coll. of Surgeons, Edin.; dist. med. offr., St. V., 1935 ; supt. physician, col. hosp., Grenada, 1952.

ALFORD, R. E., C.M.G.—b. 1904 ; ed. Winchester and Univ. Coll., Oxford; nav. serv., 1940-45 ; cadet, Nig., 1928 ; asst. dist. offr., 1931 ; dist. offr. (clk., exec. and leg. couns.), 1938 ; senr. dist. offr., 1946 ; fin. sec., Zanz., 1947 ; ch. sec., 1952.
ALLAN, W., O.B.E.—b. 1904 ; ed. North Kelvinside Sch., Glasgow Univ. and I.C.T.A.; asst. research offr., agric. dept., N. Rhod., 1928 ; asst. dir., agric. and entom., 1938 ; dir., Maur., 1948.
ALLEN, D. M., M.B.E. (1949).—b. 1916 ; ed. Bloxham and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; cadet, admin., G.C., 1938 ; asst. dist. comsnr., 1939 ; dist. comsnr., 1945 ; admin. offr., cl. II, 1950.

ALLEN, E. C., O.B.E.—b. 1902 ; ed. Bradford Commercial Sch. and Sch. of Accountancy; ck., secretariat and reporter, leg. co., Nyasa., 1925 ; asst. treas., Tang., 1930 ; senr., 1940 ; second. as treas., ch. of customs, British Som., 1940 ; fin. sec. and collr. of inc. tax, Basuto., 1945.

ALLEN, J. G. C.—b. 1904 ; ed. Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, Ecole Superieure de Commerce, Lausanne, and Munich Univ.; mil. serv., 1940-43, maj.; cadet, Nig., 1926 ; senr. dist. offr., 1945 ; res., 1947 ; Br. comsnr., Anglo-French Cameroons Boundary Coms., 1937-39 ; Nig. gov. rep. to Free French, Duala, 1940 ; i/c. mil. intell. and censorship, Nig., 1940-41 ; liaison offr. to Gen. Leclerc, 1942 ; mem., res. min's staff, Accra, 1943 ; labour offr., 1943-44 ; offl., "goodwill" visit, Algeria and Morocco, 1949.

ALLEN, J. W. T.—b. 1904 ; ed. Westminster and St. John's Coll., Oxford; supt., educ., T.T., 1929 ; asst. dist. offr., 1937 ; dist. offr., 1942 ; political offr., Aden, 1947 ; dep. Br. agt., W. Aden Prot., 1948.

ALLEYNE, K. H. C.—b. 1918 ; ed. Dominica Gram. Sch., Univ. Coll., London, and Queen's Coll., Oxford (Devonshire 2nd course); barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, called to bar, 1939 ; registr., Dominica, 1943 ; mag., St. L., 1944 ; cr. atty., 1949 ; St. V., 1952 ; author of Memoir of constitutional development of St. Lucia, 1950.
ALLIN, P. W. M.—b. 1906 ; ed. Hilton Coll., Natal, and Rhodes Univ., Cape; land survr., N. Rhod., 1930 ; senr. survr., 1945 ; dep. dir., surv. and land, 1951.

AMOROSO-CENTENO, A. E., B., M.B.E.—b. 1910 ; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Port-of-Spain; called to bar, Gray's Inn; clk., postal, treas. and acctnt.-gen's depts., Trin., 1928-42 ; assessor, inl. rev. dept., 1943 ; sec., 1950 ; dep. comsnr., 1952.

ANDERSON, J. W. S.—b. 1898 ; ed. Daniel Stewart's Coll., Heriot-Watt Coll., and Edin. Univ.; mil. serv., 1917-19, 1942-45 ; asst. engrn., F.M.S., 1925 ; exec. engrn., 1934 ; p.a. to dir.-advsr., S'pore, 1935 ; state engrn., gr. II, 1947 ; gr. I, 1948 ; D.D.P.W., Mal., 1950.

ANDERSON, T. F., O.B.E. (mil.).—b. 1901 ; ed. Rugby, Trinity Hall, Camb., and Univ. Coll. Hosp.; mil. serv., 1939-46, col. (desps.) ; med. offr., Ken., 1928 ; D.M.S., Som., 1946 ; D.D.M.S., Ken., 1949 ; D.M.S., 1949 ; author of various papers on medicine and health in E. Africa.
ANDRE, J. A. H., M.B.E.—b. 1898 ; ed. Primary Sch., Maur., Royal Coll., Maur., and Lond. Sch. of Trop. Med.; govt. med. offr., 1924 ; D.D.M.S., Maur., 1951.
ANGUS, J. R. B.—b. 1912; ed. Malvern Coll., Glas. High Sch. and Edin. Univ.; mil. serv., 1940-41; lt.; asst. consv. forests, Ken., 1940-50; consv. forests, Fiji, 1951.

APTHORP, C. H. F., M.V.O., C.P.M.—b. 1904; tpr., Swaz. police, 1924; asst. comsnr., police, Nig., 1927; comsnr. (later senr. asst. supt.), 1936; supt. (later asst. comsnr.), S.L., 1944; comsnr., mounted police, Basuto., 1946; comsnr., police, Nyasa., 1949.

ARCHER, C. V. H.—b. 1904; ed. Harrison Coll., Barb., and Camb. Univ.; barrister-at-law (Gray's Inn); clk. to atty.-gen., Barb., 1930; police mag., Barb., 1935; judge, Bridgetown petty debt ct., 1938; leg. draughtsman, Trin., 1944; jt. author, 1944, rev. edn. of Laws of Barb.
ARCHER, E. W. D.—b. 1893; ed. Harrison Coll., Barb.; rev. offr., St. V., 1917; asst. mstr., St. Mary's Coll., St. L., 1918; Grenada Boys' Sec. Sch., 1929; hdmstr., Sec. Sch., Montserrat.

ARCHER, W. S.—b. 1907; ed. The Lodge Sch., Barb.; clk. to the admistr., Dominica, 1931; treas., Antigua, 1941; emergency offr., 1942; fed. supply offr., 1945; asst. col. sec., 1946; prin. asst. sec., Trin., 1948.

ARDEN-CLARKE, Sir Charles, G.C.M.G. (1952), K.C.M.G. (1948), Kt. (1946), C.M.G. (1941).—b. 1898; ed. Rossall Sch. and Emmanuel Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1917-20, capt.; admin. offr., Nig., 1920; asst. res. comsnr. and govt. sec., Bech., 1936; res. comsnr., 1937; Basuto., 1942; gov. and c-in-c., Sarawak, 1946; G.C., 1949.

ARDILL, R. H.—b. 1911; ed. Kilkenny Coll., Mountjoy Sch. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; mil. serv., 1942; history mstr., Royal Coll., Maur., 1936; public rel. offr., 1946; dep. dir. educ., 1951; author of School Geography of Mauritius.

ARMITAGE, R. P., C.M.G., M.B.E.—b. 1906; ed. Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; cadet, Ken., 1929; sec. to mem. for agric., animal husb. and nat. resources, 1945; admin. sec., 1947; und. sec., G.C., 1948; fin. sec., 1948; min. of fin., 1951.

ARMSTRONG, G. J., C.B.E. (1946).—b. 1901; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown; clk., secretariat, Basuto., 1920; asst. dist. comsnr., 1936; dist. comsnr., 1938; finan. sec., Swaz., 1940; dep. res. comsnr. and govt. sec., Basuto., 1948.
ARMSTRONG, H. C.—b. 1906; ed. Dungannon Royal Sch. and Queen's Univ., Belfast; mil. serv., 1939-43; med. offr., G.C., 1934; M.O.H., 1937; asst. dir., med. services, 1949.
ARMSTRONG, H. H.—b. 1902; ed. Marlborough Coll.; cadet, Tang., 1926; asst. comsnr., labour, Maur., 1940; prov. labour offr., Nyasa., 1948; senr. labour offr., 1949; labour adviser, 1952.
ARMSTRONG, R., O.B.E. (1953).—b. 1906; clk., Swaz., 1925; asst. dist. comsnr., 1931; dist. comsnr., 1936; first asst. sec., 1951.

ARNE, Miss B. L.—b. 1900; ed. S.A. and Ashby de la Zouche Girls' Gram. Sch.; min. food and min. supply, 1939; asst. soc. welf. offr., Barb., 1945; soc. welf. offr., 1949; org. sec., nat. jt. comtee. for Spanish relief, 1939.

ARNOLD, R. M.—b. 1918; ed. Cranbrook Sch. and Royal Vet. Coll., Lond., 1938-42; advisory service, min. of agric., 1942; seconded to J'ca as vet. invest. offr., 1946; dep. dir. of agric. (livestock), J'ca, 1950; repd. J'ca at several techl. conferences; author of Tick Control Measures.

ARROWSMITH, E. P., C.M.G. (1950).—b. 1909; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. comsnr., Bech. Prot., 1933, resigned 1938; comsnr., Turks and Caicos Is., 1940; adminr., Dominica, 1946; res. comsnr., Basuto., 1951.
ARTHUR, O. R.—b. 1905; ed. Charterhouse and Corpus Christi Coll., Camb.; cadet, Nig., 1928; admin. offr., Cyp., 1937; comsnr., 1946; ch. comsnr., 1948; col. sec., Berm., 1951.

ARUNDELL, Brig. Sir Robert, K.C.M.G. (1950), C.M.G. (1947), O.B.E. (mil.) (1943), K.St.J.—b. 1904; ed. Blundell's Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1941-45 (desps., O.B.E.); cadet, Tang., 1927; seconded to C.O., 1935-37; asst. ch. sec., Uga., 1939; ch. civ. affrs. offr., M.E.F., 1944; Br. res. mem. in Washington of Caribbean comsnr., 1946; gov. and c-in-c., Wind. Is., 1948; Barb., 1952; sec., Nyasa. fin. comsnr., 1937-38.
ASH, W. G.—b. 1918; ed. Dover County Sch.; nav. serv., 1939-46; asst. P.R.O., S.L., 1946; P.R.O., 1950.

ASHMORE, J. H.—b. 1900; mil. serv., 1918-19 and 1940-41; sub-inspr., police, B. Guiana, 1920; dist. inspr., 1923; travelling mag. and warden, nos. 3 and 4 mining dists., 1923-24; county inspr., 1927; local comdt., C.I.D., Cyp., 1932; dep. comsnr., police, 1935; comsnr., 1940; air raids comdt., 1940-44; comsnr., Cyp. dist., St. John Ambulance bde.
ASKWITH, T. G.—b. 1911; ed. Haileybury and Cambridge; dist. offr., Ken., 1936; municipal African affrs. offr., 1945; comsnr., community development, 1950.

ASTLEY, B. A.—b. 1902; ed. St. Albans Sch. and Birkbeck Coll., Univ of Lond.; mil. serv., 1940, capt.; asst. mstr., educ. dept., Ken., 1929; inspr., schs., 1935; hdmstr., Prince of Wales Sch., 1938; ch. inspr., schs., 1944; dep. dir., educ., 1951.
ASTON, F. W.—b. 1903; ed. Kingsmead Sch., Hoylake, Liverpool Univ., Royal "Dick" Vet. Coll., Edin., and Manchester Univ.; vet. offr., Ken., 1928; senr., 1946; D.D.V.S., G.C., 1949.
ATKINS, C. F.—b. 1907; ed. Blundell's Sch., Oundle Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; cadet, Ken., 1930; senr. dist. comsnr., 1950.

ATKINSON, J. B., K.P.M. (1950).—b. 1907; ed. Perrins Sch. and Imp. Coll., Lond.; interned 1942-45; police cadet (chart. co. serv.), N. Borneo, 1929; asst. supt., armed constab., 1933; supt., 1936; comdt., N.B.A.C. and col. police serv., 1946; title changed to comsnr., police, 1950.

ATKINSON, J. C.—b. 1895; ed. Addingham High Prov. Sch., Ilkley Gram. Sch. and Midland Rly.; traff. inspr., govt. rly., J'ca, 1920; ch. clk. traff. br., 1930; traff. supt., 1938; traff. man., 1943; gen. man., 1949.

ATTENBOROUGH, J. P., O.B.E. (1946).—b. 1901; ed. Manchester Gram. Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; supt. educ., Nig., 1924; lectr. and senr. inspr., Pal., 1930; dir., educ., Aden, 1937; dep. dir., Pal., 1946; asst. educ. advr., C.O., 1948; dir., educ., Tang, 1948.
AUSTIN, A. J.—b. 1907; ed. Clifton Coll.; asst. treas., N. Rhod., 1930; acctnt., 1938; dep. acctnt.-gen., 1939; fin. sec., Swaz., 1945; dep. acctnt.-gen., N. Rhod., 1950; acctnt.-gen., 1951.
AZIZ, A.—b. 1899; ed. Turkish Sch. and English Sch., Nicosia; student clk., Cyp., 1918; asst. P.M.G., 1947; P.M.G., 1950.
BABBI, B. A.—b. 1907; ed. Bedford Modern Sch. and St. John's Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1941-45, lt.-cmdt.; supt., educ., Nig., 1929; educ. offr., Tang., 1945; dir., educ., Zanz., 1951.

BACKHOUSE, M. V., M.B.E. (mil.).—b. 1901; ed. Durham Sch. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1941-45, maj.; cadet, Nig., 1925; senr. dist. offr., 1944; res., admin. serv., cl. I, 1946; senr. res., 1951.

BACON, R. S., M.B.E. (1943).—b. 1895; ed. Rugby and Balliol Coll., Oxford; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1923; mil. serv., 1914-19 and (staff list) 1940-46, col.; pvt. prac., London, 1923-39; ch. just., Gib., 1946; carried out police enquiry, Gib., 1946; author of Commercial Arbitrations.
BAILEY, A. R.—b. 1906; ed. Merchant Taylor's Sch., Univ. Coll., Lond. Univ. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; cadet, Nig., 1929; admin. offr., cl. II, 1947; admin. sec., N. region, 1952.
BAILEY, C. E. S.—b. 1896; ed. St. Kitts Gram. Sch. and Edin. Univ.; govt. M.O., Antigua, 1933; asst. D.M.S. (curative), B. Guiana, 1950.
BAILEY, F. W.—b. 1902; ed. Davenport High Sch.; asst. storekeeper, P.W.D., Nig., 1928; storekeeper, 1937; ch., 1943; contrlr. stores and trans., N. Rhod., 1949.
BAIN, I. C. I.—b. 1896; ed. Grenada Boys' Secondary Sch.; clk., Grenada, 1918; cust. and excise offr., 1924; 2nd clk., treas., 1931; inspr., inc. tax., 1939; compt., 1944.

BAIRAMIAN, V. R.—b. 1900; ed. English Sch., Nicosia, Cyp., and Univ. Coll., Lond.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1923; clk., dist. ct., Cyp., 1926; asst. registr., sup. ct., 1927; registr. dist. ct., 1929; also official recvr. in bankruptcy, 1931-39; ch. registr., sup. ct., 1931; asst. dir., land registn., 1938; legal asst., lands and survey dept., Nig., 1944; ch. registr., sup. ct., 1944; mag., 1946; puisne judge, 1949; sec., usury enq. comtee., Cyp., 1926-28; asst. to atty.-gen., for revision of lands law, 1943.

BAIRD, D. A.—b. 1909; ed. Glasgow Acad., Geo. Watson's Coll., Edin., and Edin. Univ.; med. offr. i/c Church of Scotland Mission Hosp., Blantyre, Nyasa., 1933; col. med. serv., 1943; S.M.O., Som., 1949; D.M.S., 1950.
BAKER, S. G.—b. 1902; ed. Abber Sch., S. Wales, and Royal Scot. Regimental Sch.; sgt. instr. (local sgt. maj.), Trin., 1929-35; asst. supt., prisons, 1933; supt., prisons, B. Guiana, 1945.
BAKER-BEALL, R. W. C., M.B.E.—b. 1903; ed. Sherborne Sch., Dorset, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; admin. offr., Ken., 1927; fin. sec., Zanz., 1942; dep. fin. sec., Nig., 1947.

BALDERAMOS, L. C.—b. 1920; ed. Holy Redeemer Elem. Sch., St. John's Coll., Belize, Wireless Sch., Calgary, Can., various airports in U.K. (airport managers' course), and Watchfield Sch. of Air Traff. Cont.; mil. serv., 1942-47, warrant offr.; airport manager, B. Hond., 1948.

BALDWIN, J. H.—b. 1902; jnr. clk., K.U.R. & H., 1915; trav. inspr. of acctts., 1925; asst. acctnt., 1926; senr. asst., 1931; expdt. acctnt., 1939; ch. asst. to gen. man., 1942; ch. acctnt., E.A.R. & H., 1948.

BALLENTINE, J. S., Maj. Gen., C.B., C.I.E.—b. 1897; ed. Portora Royal Sch. and R.M.C.; commissioned Indian Army, 1916; q.m.g., India, 1947; def. sec., E.A.H.C., 1949; spec. comsnr., lands, Ken., 1951.

BAMFORD, J. B.—b. 1903; ed. Regent Street Poly. and Northampton Inst., Lond.; govt. survr., 1937; dist. comsnr., 1943; comsnr. of the interior, 1950; survr., Br. Guiana-Brazil bound. comsn., 1935; secon. col. dev. corp., 1948-50.

BANNISTER, A. A.—b. 1894; ed. Codrington Coll. and Rawle Training Inst., Barb.; spec. war serv., 1915-16; asst. inspr., schs., B. Guiana, 1924; asst. mstr., teach. train. coll., 1928; 2nd inspr., schs., 1937; inspr. 1939; prin., govt. train. coll., 1945; ch. inspr. and asst. dir., educ., 1948; redesig. dep. dir., educ., 1949; dir., 1952.

BANTING, W. V.—b. 1903; ed. Haileybury Coll.; asst. treas., Tang., 1930; senr. asst. treas., 1940; acctnt.-gen., comsnr., incl. rev., comsnr., stamp duty, govt. storekeeper, Fiji, 1940; comsnr. of currency; dep. acctnt.-gen., N. Rhod., 1951.
BARCROFT, J. C. H.—b. 1908; ed. Weymouth Coll.; cadet, Sarawak, 1930; dist. offr., 1939; civ. intern., 1941-45; res., 1946; Br. res., Brunei, 1951; senr. res., 1952.
BARING, C. C.—b. 1898; ed. Eton; mil. serv., both World Wars, major; Prison Serv., 1930; Home Offr., 1946-49; prob. offr., Berm., 1951; warden of prisons, 1952.

BARING, Hon. Sir Evelyn, K.C.M.G., K.C.V.O., K.St.J., late I.C.S.—b. 1903; ed. Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; entered I.C.S., 1926; sec. to Agent of Govt. of India in S. Africa, 1929; retd., 1934; gov., S. Rhod., 1942-44; high comsnr. for U.K. in Union of S.A., and high comsnr. for Basuto., the Bech. Prot. and Swaz., 1944-51; gov. and c-in-c., Ken., 1952.
BARKER, J. E.—b. 1910; ed. Regent Poly. and Jesus Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1942-46; asst. audr., Ken., 1934; S.S. and F.M.S., 1938; senr. asst., G.C., 1946; dep. audr., later dep. dir., aud., 1947.

MUNDY, J. C., C.M.G. (1947).—b. 1900; ed. Varndean, Brighton; war serv., 1918 (R.A.F.); astt. inspr., taxes, U.K., 1920; inspr., 1922; comsnt., inland rev., Ken., 1937; comsnt., inc. tax, E.A., 1940; introd. inc. tax, Ken., 1937; Tang., Uga. and Zanz., 1940; fin. mem., E.A.H.C., 1948.
MUNIR, M. N.—b. 1923; ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; cr. coun., Tang., 1949; solr.-gen., Cyp., 1952.
MUNROE, E. L.—b. 1904; ed. Calabar High Sch.; entered J'ca govt. serv., 1922; assessor, 1943; prin., 1944; astt. comsnt., inc. tax and stamp duties, 1946; dep. comsnt., 1946.

MUNSTER, 5th Earl of.—b. 1906; ed. Charterhouse; paymr.-gen., 1938; par. U.S. of S. for War, Feb.-Sep., 1939; A.D.C. and mil. asst. to Gen. Viscount Gort, V.C., 1939-41; G.S.O. 2, Malta, 1942; capt. grenadier guards; par. U.S. of S. for India and Burma, 1943-44; H.O. 1944-45; par. U.S. of S., cols., since 1951.

MURPHY, J. P.—b. 1909; ed. Mount St. Mary's Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; barrister-at-law, King's Inn, Dublin; mil. serv., 1941-45; astt. cr. solr., H.K., 1936; atty.-gen., Gam., 1947; Zanz., 1950; M.E.C. and M.L.C.

MURPHY, R. H.—b. 1915; ed. Charterhouse and Emmanuel Coll., Camb.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; mil. serv., 1939-46; res. mag., Tang., 1948; ch. registr., G.C., 1951; registr., W.A. ct. of appeal, 1951.
MURRAY, J. P.—b. 1906; ed. St. Edward's Sch., Oxford, and Christ Church, Oxford; cadet, N. Rhod., 1929; dist. offr., 1931; prov. comsnt., 1950.
MURRAY, R. E. K.—b. 1902; clk., gr. II, Basuto., 1924; sub-inspr., Basuto., M.P., 1930; astt. dist. comsnt., 1937; dist. comsnt., 1939.

MURRAY-AVNSLEY, Sir Charles, Kt., Bach. (1950).—b. 1893; ed. Marlborough, St. Paul's and St. John's Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1915-19; lieut.; dist. comsnr., B. Hond., 1927; ch. just., Tonga, 1930; Grenada, 1935; puisne judge, S.S., 1938; judge, Ipoh, 1938; ch. just., S'pore, 1946.

MUSPRATT-WILLIAMS, M. J.—b. 1911; ed. Imp. Service Coll., R.M.C., Sandhurst; mil. serv., 1931-46; (from 1936-45, R.A.F., gp. capt.) lt.-col.; dir., civil aviation, N. Rhod., 1947; H.K., 1952; chmn., S.A. air trans. coun., 1951.

NASMYTH, Comdr. G. B., J.P.—nav. serv., 1915-18; lieut.; harbour mstr. and marine survr., Levuka, Fiji, 1930; harbour mstr. and govt. meteorologist, survr. to Lloyds, Suva, Fiji, 1935; comdr., Fiji N.V.F., 1939; comdng. offr., F.N.V.R., 1940; capt. of the port, Gib., 1944.
NEAL, W. J. L., O.B.E.—b. 1903; ed. Queen's Univ., Belfast; Ind. Med. Serv., 1926; ret., 1949; med. supt., Aden, 1948; D.M.S., N. Borneo, 1951.

NEERUNJUN, R., O.B.E.—b. 1906; ed. Royal Coll., Maur., Lond. Sch. of Econ., King's Coll. and Univ. NEFDT, H. M.—b. 1899; laby. asst., Ken., 1926; gov't. chem., 1946.

NEVILLE, Major-General Sir Robert, K.C.M.G., C.B.E., R.M.—b. 1896; ed. NEWBIGGING, J. H.—b. 1908; ed. Ardrossan Acad. and Strathallan Sch.; p.o.w., 1942-45; acctnt., surv. dept., Mal., 1936; acctnt., public trustee, 1937; mil. fin., 1940; senr. treas. acctnt., 1948; settlement treas., Penang, 1948; dep. acctnt.-gen., 1950.

NEWBOLD, C. D., Q.C.—b. 1909; ed. Lodge Sch., Barb., and Keble Coll., Oxford; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1931; prin. offr. and 2nd dep. marshal, sup. ct. registry, Trin., 1936; mag., 1937; leg. draftsman, J'ca, 1941; solr.-gen., 1943; leg. sec., E.A.H.C., 1948; mem. of coms. of enq. into land taxn., 1942; repd. NEWBOLT, Capt. A. F., C.M.G. (1949).—b. 1893; ed. Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; war serv., 1914-19, adjt.; asst. p.s. (appts.) to S. of S., Lord Milner, 1919; Mr. Winston Churchill, 1921, Duke of Devonshire, 1922, Mr. J. H. Thomas, 1924, Mr. L. C. M. S. NEWING, W. T.—b. 1895; ed. Gravesend Sec. Sch.; mil. serv., 1915-17; clk., B/T., 1910; Br. P.O., 1920; asst. contrlr.-gen., contr. coms. for Germany, 1945-46; ch. acctnt., Ken., Uga. and Tang., P. & T. dept., 1946; asst. P.M.G. (finance) and ch. acctnt., E.A.P. & T., 1952.

NEWLAND, C. H., O.B.E.—b. 1906; ed. Manchester Gram. Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford, law student, Gray's Inn; asst. dist. coms., S.L., 1929; A.D.C. and p.s. to the gov', S.L., 1934-36; dist. coms., 1936; asst. ch. sec., Aden, 1945; dev. coms., Maur., 1952.

NEWLIN, T. E.—b. 1898; ed. Reigate Gram. Sch. and Handsworth Theol. Coll., Birm.; ordained minister, Methodist Church; mil. serv., 1916-18, sgt.; sec., social welf. servs., J'ca, 1943; soc. welf. advsr.
NEWNS, A. F. F. P.—b. 1909; ed. Christ's Hosp. and St. Catharine's Coll., Camb.; admin. serv., Nig., 1932; cl. I., 1951; sec. to coun. of ministers, 1951.

NICHOLSON, R. A.—b. 1905; ed. Blundell's Sch. and King's Coll., Lond. Univ.; asst. engr., P.W.D., Mal., 1928; cadet, N. Rhod., 1931; dist. offr., 1933; asst. ch. sec., 1945; econ. sec., 1948.

NICOLL, Sir John Fearns, K.C.M.G. (1953), C.M.G. (1946).—b. 1899; ed. Carlisle Gram. Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1917-19, lieut.; cadet, B. N. NOAKES, J. L., M.B.E.—b. 1907; ed. NORRIS, C. W.—b. 1911; ed. Colfe Gram. Sch.; N. Rhod., 1930; asst. assessor, 1938; assessor, H.K., 1948; asst. comsnr., incl. rev., 1951.

NUTMAN, F. J., O.B.E. (1953).—b. 1904; ed. Newton Abbot Sch. and Univ. of Lond. (Imp. Coll.); mycologist, forest prod. lab., 1926; plant physiologist, E.A.A.R.S., Amani, 1928; secon. NYE, G. W., O.B.E.—b. 1902; ed. Forest Sch., Wye Agric. Coll., Imperial Coll. of Sci., Cambridge Univ., and I.C.T.A.; cotton botanist, Uga., 1924; dep. dir., agric., 1940; dir., agric., Nyasa, 1945 (prot. comsnr. for scouts); dep. agric. advsr., C.O., 1948.

OAKELEY, R. H.—b. 1909; ed. Clifton Coll., Oxford Univ.; interned, 1942-45; cadet, M.C.S., 1931; studied Hokkien in China, 1932-33; Chinese prot., S'pore, 1934; asst. prot., Selangor, 1939; dep. contrlr., labour (Chinese), S'pore, 1941; dep. comsnr., labour, 1947; dir., govt. offrs' language sch., 1951; ag. sec., Chinese affrs., Mal., 1952.

OBRIEN, B. J., C.M.G. (1950).—b. 1902; ed. Uppingham and Queen's Coll., Oxford; asst. sec., col. sec.'s off., Cyp., 1927; clk., exec. coun., 1931; admin. offr., asst. sec., 1935; comsnr., 1936; asst. col. sec., Maur., 1939; prin. asst. col. sec., 1943; under-sec., Trin., 1943; col. sec., Gib., 1947; jt. ed. O'CONNOR, J., M.B.E. (1947)—b. 1896; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trin.; clk., Trin., 1914; asst. col. sec., 1935; und. sec., 1947; gov't sec., and clk. of ex. coun., 1950.

O'CONNOR, Sir Kenneth, M.C.—b. 1896; ed. St. Columba's Coll., Ireland; mil. serv., 1915-18 and 1945, col. OSBORNE, N. D.—b. 1915; ed. Harrison Coll., Barb.; cadet, port health dept., Barb., 1940; inspr., taxes, B. Guiana, 1949; asst. comsnr., income tax, Barb., 1951.

Cathedral Sch., Hereford; served on U.K. rlws., 1922-27; dist. engrn., J'ca rlwy., 1928; asst. engrn., P.W.D., J'ca, 1938; res. engrn., 1940; exec. engrn., P.W.D., Trin.; maintenance engrn., rlws., 1943; operating supt., 1944; gen. man., rlws., Trin., 1944; B. PETRIE, E. J.—b. 1907; asst. rev. offr., Ken., 1933; asst. treas., 1934; senr. acctnt., 1943; asst. fin. sec., 1946; fin. sec., Barb., 1948; acctnt.-gen., Ken., 1951; sec., treasy., 1953.
PHILLIPS, D.—b. 1905; ed. St. James Select Sch., Liverpool; prison offr., H.M. prison comsn., Eng. and Wales, 1932; supt., prisons, N. Rhod., 1946; dep. comsnr., prisons, 1948.

PHILLIPS, E. J.—b. 1908; ed. elem. sch., Birsay, sec. sch., Stromness, Orkney, and Aberdeen Univ.; war serv., 1942 and 1945; asst. registr., stats., S.S. and F.M.S., 1932; dep. registr., Penang, 1939-40; dep. contrlr., restricted imports in addn., 1939-42; dep. supt., rubber control, and dep. registr., textiles, in addn., 1934-42; secon. for duties with supply branch secretariat, Nig., 1934-44; M.P.U., 1945; S.O. II, trade and industry, S'pore divn., B.M.A., 1945; registr., Mal. stats., S'pore, 1946.

PHILLIPS, H. E. I., M.B.E. (mil.) (1946).—b. 1914; ed. Haberdashers' Sch., and Univ. Coll., Lond.; mil. serv., 1939-42; cadet, Nyasa, 1946; asst. dist. comsnr., 1948; ag. dev. sec., 1950; dev. sec. (supnmry.), 1952.

PHILLIPS, H. R.—b. 1901; ed. Whitgift Sch. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; supt., educ., N. Nig., 1924; senr. educ. offr., Nig., 1938; temp. dir., educ., S.L., 1945-46; ch. inspr., educ., N.P., Nig., 1946; dep. dir., N. region, 1950; dir., 1951.

PHILLIPS, I. L.—b. 1910; ed. Worksop Coll., Selwyn Coll., Camb., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; admin. serv., G.C., 1934; seconded to Pal., 1938; admin. offr., Cyp., 1948; cl. II, cl. IC, M.C.S., 1951; asst. sec., off. of comsnr.-gen., S.E. Asia, 1951.

PHILLIPSON, Sir Sydney, Kt. Bach., C.M.G. (1946).—b. 1892; ed. Accrington and Manchester Gram. Schs. and Manchester Univ.; mil. serv., 1915-19, lieut.; cadet, Ceylon, 1919; various judicial and admin. posts, 1919-40; dep. fin. sec., 1940; fin. sec., Uga., 1941; Nig., 1945; engaged on special enq. into fin. relations between govt. and native admin. in Nig., 1946.
PICKERING, B. H.—b. 1894; ed. St. Paul's Cath. Choir Sch. and H.M.S. Worcester; mil. serv., 1916-19, capt.; apptd. col. serv., 1939; marine supt. and harbourmstr., Kingston, J'ca, 1943.
PICKETT, F. H.—b. 1906; ed. Grey Coll. Sch., Port Elizabeth, and Rhodes Univ. Coll., Grahamstown; inspr. of schls., Basuto., 1927; hdmstr., Basuto. High Sch., 1939; dir. of educ., 1948.
PIERCY, S. E.—b. 1909; ed. Sedbergh Sch., Durham Univ. and Royal Vet. Coll.; vet. res. offr., Ken., 1939; senr., 1947; ch., 1952.

PIERS, E. S. F.—b. 1903; ed. Nova Scotia Pub. Schs., Dalhousie Univ., and Nova Scotia Tech. Coll.; mil. serv., 1942-43, capt.; asst. engrn., P.W.D., B. Guiana, 1937; dist. engrn., 1943; exec., engrn., 1946; D.D.P.W., 1951; mem. stand. comte., employ. exchange serv.

PIKE, A. H., O.B.E. (1945).—b. 1903; ed. The Abbey, Tipperary, Trinity Coll., Dublin, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; cadet, Tang., 1927; asst. dist. offr., 1930; dist. offr., 1938; dep. prov. comsnr., 1947; prov. comsnr., 1948; senr. prov. comsnr., 1951.

PINARD, L. A.—b. 1912; ed. Dominica Gram. Sch.; Devonshire 2nd Course, Oxford, 1949; clk. var. depts., 1933; clk. of couns., 1946; contrlr., supplies, 1947; asst. govt. sec., 1948; govt. sec., 1951; asst. admnr., Dominica, 1952.

PLEASS, C. J., C.M.G. (1950).—b. 1901 ; ed. The Royal Masonic Sch. and Selwyn Coll., Camb. ; cadet, Nig., 1924 ; res., 1945 ; senr. res., 1946 ; dev. sec., 1947 ; lt.-gov., E. region, 1951.

PLUMRIDGE, H. F. P.—b. 1905 ; ed. Ealing County Sch. and Lond. Sch. of Econ. ; asst. acctnt., Nig. rlyw., 1936 ; admin. asst., 1940 ; asst. staff man., 1946 ; asst. gen. man. (staff and orgn.), 1950 ; mem., govt. comttee. on daily rates of pay, 1947 ; assessor to arb. trib. in trade disputes, 1945 and 1948.

PLUNKETT, E. O.—b. 1908 ; ed. Ireland and Douai Sch., Berks ; Pal. police, 1933-37 ; supt., police, and asst. supt., fire brigade, Barb., 1937-42 ; senr. supt., police, and comdt., local forces, St. L., 1942-47 ; supt., special br., N. Borneo, 1947 ; dep. comsnr., 1949.
POLLETT, J. D., O.B.E. (1952).—b. 1902 ; ed. Christ's Coll., Finchley, Royal Sch. of Mines and Imp. Coll. of Sci. and Tech. ; asst. geol., S.L., 1928 ; geol., 1939 ; dir., geol. surv., 1950.

PORCHER, M. S.—b. 1921 ; ed. Cheltenham, and St. Edmund Hall, Oxford ; mil. serv., 1941-42, lieut. ; admin. cadet, S.L., 1942 ; asst. dist. comsnr., 1945 ; dist. comsnr., 1951 ; dep. col. sec., B. Guiana, 1952.
POTTER, H. S., C.M.G.—b. 1904 ; ed. Shrewsbury, and Queens' Coll., Camb. ; cadet, Ken., 1926 ; dist. offr., 1928 ; dep. fin. sec., 1944 ; fin. sec., Uga., 1945 ; ch. sec., 1948 ; Ken., 1952.

POTTER, R. C. G.—b. 1899 ; ed. Queen's Coll., B. Guiana, and Mico Training Coll., J'ca. ; asst. mstr., teachers' training centre, B. Guiana, 1933 ; county inspr., schl., 1943 ; inspr., schl., 1945 ; prin., govt. train. coll., 1948 ; dep. dir., educ., 1952.

POTTINGER, J. H.—b. 1898 ; ed. Sec. Sch. for Boys, Bootle, Lancs., and Univ. of Liverpool ; mil. serv., 1915-18, 2nd lieut. ; M.O.H., G.C., 1928 ; asst. govt. M.O.H., B. Guiana, 1932 ; S.M.O., Pal., 1935 ; Nig., 1947 ; asst. D.M.S., 1948 ; D.M.S., W. regn., 1951.
POUND, G. B.—b. 1911 ; mil. serv. ; apptd. (temp.), G.C., 1936 ; asst. supt., govt. press, 1938 ; supt., 1938 ; govt. printer, 1951.

POYNTON, Sir Hilton, K.C.M.G. (1949), C.M.G. (1946).—b. 1905 ; ed. U.S. of S., 1946 ; dep. U.S. of S., 1948.

PRENDERGAST, M.—b. 1918 ; ed. St. Mungo's Acad., Glasgow Univ. and Jordanhill Training Coll. ; mil. serv., 1939-40 ; hd. and dep. hd., Colegio Markham, Peru, 1946 ; inspr. and gen. supt. of schs., Bah., 1948.

PRETHEROE, E. O., M.C.—b. 1896 ; ed. privately and St. John's Coll., Camb. ; barrister-at-law ; mil. serv., 1914-19 ; asst. sec., Nig., 1922 ; police mag., 1928 ; cr. coun., 1930 ; atty.-gen., B. Guiana, 1939 ; puisne judge, Mal., 1946.
PREIOUS, C. B., M.B.E.—b. 1906 ; ed. St. Mark's Sch., Mbabane, Swaz. ; Swaz. treas., 1927 ; acctnt. and asst. colltr., inc. tax, Basuto., 1940 ; senr. acctnt., N. Borneo, 1949 ; acctnt.-gen., 1952.

PREIOUS, Maj. L. P.—b. 1908 ; ed. St. Mark's Sch., Mbabane, Swaz., and Carolina High Sch., Transvaal ; trooper, Swaz. police, 1928 ; L/C., 1934 ; sergeant, 1937 ; asst. supt., 1938 ; supt., 1944 ; dep. comsnr., 1952.

PRICE, R. M. R.—b. 1917 ; ed. St. John's Coll., Belize ; mil. serv., 1942-46, capt. ; asst. supt. police, B. Hond., 1947.

PUGH, I. W.—b. 1910 ; ed. Gram. Sch., Dolgelly ; mil. serv., 1936-47 ; exec. engnr., Tang., gr. II, 1946 ; gr. I, 1947 ; asst. D.P.W., 1950 ; D.P.W., G.C., 1952.

PURCELL, J. F. B., M.B.E.—b. 1910; cl., Swaz., 1929; asst. dist. comsnr., 1937; dist. offr., 1948.

PUTSEY, C. W.—b. 1897; ed. St. Clement's Ch. Sch., York.; mil. serv., 1915-19; traff. inspr., Nig., 1927; asst. traff. offr., 1936; senr. asst., 1944; dist. traff. supt., 1946; asst. traff. man., 1949; ch. supt., Nig. rly., 1951.

QUASHIE-IDUN, S. O.—b. 1902; ed. Mfantsipim Sch., Cape Coast, and Selwyn Coll., Camb.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; dist. mag., G.C., 1936; puisne judge, 1948; mem. of the comsn. of enq. into the disturbance at Enugu, Nig., 1949.
QUENNELL, A. M.—b. 1906; ed. Otago Boys' Sch. and Univ., and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; mil. serv., 1940-45; capt.; senr. geologist, Tang., 1948; asst. dir., geol. surv., 1952.
QUIGLEY, J. T. D.—b. 1903; asst. acctnt., Bech. Prot., 1930-48; senr. to Swaz.; ag. fn. sec., 1949; treas., 1950.

QUINN-YOUNG, C. T.—b. 1904; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; supt., educ., S.P., Nig., 1926; senr. educ. offr., Nig., 1938; asst. dir. of educ., S.P., 1944; since regraded dep. dir., E.P.; dir., E. region, 1951; attd. to comsn. on higher educ. in W.A. as liaison offr., 1944; jt. author of Geography of Nigeria, and of history bks. for Nig. schs. and teachers.

QUINTON, Maj. F. J., T.D. (1945).—b. 1897; ed. Nelson Sch., Gt. Yarmouth, and Fitzwilliam Hall, Camb.; war serv., 1915-20, 2nd lt.; major, r. of o., R.E. RAE, A. M. W., C.M.G. (1945), M.D., Ch.B.—b. 1896; ed. George Heriots, Edin. and Edin. Univ.; mil. serv., 1914-18; deleg. for S.L. and the Gam. to Pan African Health Conf., Jo'b'urg, 1935; M.O., Gam., 1924; S.L., 1931; S.M.O., G.C., 1933; Gam., 1935; asst. D.M.S., G.C., 1936; D.D.M.S., 1938; Nig., 1939; asst. med. advsr., C.O., 1944; dep. med. advsr., C.O., 1945; dep. chief med. offr., 1948.
RAE, N., C.B.E.—b. 1892; mil. serv., 1917-20, lt.; entd. C.A. off., 1911; asst. head of dept., 1919; dep. head, stores dept., 1932; dep. ch. clk., 1935; head, stores dept., 1945.

RAEBURN, C., C.B.E.—b. 1894; ed. Hamilton Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; mil. serv., 1915-19 and 1941, maj. (desps.); geologist, geol. survey, Nig., 1920; asst. dir., 1929; seconded to Cyp. as water engrn. in ch., water supply and irrig. dept., 1937; water engrn., Cyp., 1943; dir. geol. survey, Nig., 1947; comsnr. on spl. duties, 1949; M.E.C., Cyp., 1945-47; author of publns. on Nig. geol. and Cyp. water supply, Alluvial Prospecting.
RAGG, H. A.—b. 1904; civ. engrn. apprentice, P.W.D., Fiji, 1923; exec. engrn., 1943; D.D.P.W., 1952.
RAIKES, T. R. C.—b. 1911; ed. Central and Secondary Schs., Port Talbot, S. Wales; Br. P.O., 1928; survr., Nig., 1944; div. survr., 1949; asst. P.M.G., S.L., 1952; P.M.G., 1952.
RAMSAY, A. B.—b. 1903; ed. Sherborne Sch. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; M.C.S., 1927; cl. V, 1930; cl. IV, 1934; cl. III, 1939; cl. II, 1944; cl. IB, 1947; ag. Br. advsr., Kedah, 1951.
RAMSAY, N. J. G.—b. 1916; ed. Merchiston Castle Sch. and Edin. Univ.; nav. serv., 1940-46, lt.; admin.-gen., N. Rhod., 1947.
RAMSAY, R.—b. 1902; ed. Clydebank High Sch. and Glasgow Univ.; med. offr., G.C., 1930; M.O.H., 1938; senr. health offr., 1945; asst. D.M.S., 1947.

RANCE, Maj.-Gen. Sir Hubert, G.C.M.G. (1948), G.B.E. (1946), C.B. (1946), O.B.E. (1940).—b. 1898; ed. Wimbledon Coll. and R.M.C., Sandhurst; mil. serv., 1916-18 and 1939-45, maj-gen. (desps.); dep. dir., mil. training, W.O.; dir. of tech. training, W.O.; chief of staff to G.O.C.in-c., Western cmd., Eng., 1943-44; mag. gen. in charge of civil affrs., Burma, 1945; gov., Burma, 1946; chmn., Br. Caribbean standing closer assoc. comtee.; Br. co-chmn., Caribbean comsn., and chmn., dev. and welf. organ. in the W.I., 1948; gov. and c.-in-c., Trin., 1950.
RANDALL, J. B.—b. 1913; ed. Bedford Sch., Camb. Univ., and Royal (Dick) Vet. Coll., Edin.; mil. serv., 1940-43, lieut.; vet. offr., Uga., 1939; D.D.V.S., 1949.

RANGELEY, W. H. J.—b. 1909; ed. Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch, S.A., Plumtree Sch., S. Rhod., Rhodes Univ. Coll., S.A., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1940-43, capt.; admin. offr., Nyasa., 1934; prov. comsnr., 1952.

RANKINE, J. D., C.M.G.—b. 1907; ed. Croydon Prep. Sch., Christ Church, N.Z., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; cadet, Uga., 1931; asst. dist. comsnr., 1933; col. sec., Barb., 1945; ch. sec. Ken., 1947; Br. resdt., Zanz., 1952.

REJA, J. T.—b. 1907; ed. Royal Sch., Dungannon, Queen's Univ., Belfast, and Camb. Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., 1931; studied Hokkien at Amoy, 1931; cl. V, 1934; cl. IV, 1938; cl. III, 1943; cl. II, 1947; asst. sec., Chinese affrs. (educ.), 1948; cl. IC, 1950; cl. IB, 1951; offg. dep. pres., city coun., S'pore, 1952.

REAY, C. S. de C., O.B.E. (1950).—b. 1895; ed. privately (N.Z.); mil. serv., 1917-19; elk., Fiji, 1914; dist. comsnr., 1927; indust. rel. offr., 1940; offl. M.L.C., 1940; comsnr., labour, 1942; (spec. duty in Maur., N. Rhod., Belg. Congo, the Rand and Natal during 1939-40).
REDDAWAY, A. F. J.—b. 1916; ed. County Sch., Ilford, and Reading Univ.; admin. offr., Cyp., 1938; cl. I, 1951.
REDHEAD, G. A.—b. 1898; ed. Grenada Boys' Sec. Sch.; solr., sup. ct., Windward and Leeward Is.; dist. offr., Northern Dist. and mag., Dominica, 1944.

REDHEAD, W. A. R.—b. 1909; ed. Grenada Boys' Sec. Sch.; elk., agric. dept., Grenada, 1926; treas. and cust., 1932; elk., cl. I, audit dept., 1945; asst. soc. welf. offr., 1946; i/c publicity servs. during and after loc. disturbances, 1951; supt., prisons, 1952.

REECE, C. W.—b. 1899; ed. Harrison Coll. and Codrington Coll., Barb. and Jesus Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law; police mag., Barb., 1926; registr., 1931; mag., Nig., 1938; cr. coun., 1939; senr., 1946; puisne judge, 1949; H.K., 1952.

REECE, Sir Gerald, K.C.M.G. (1950), C.B.E. (1943), O.B.E. (1937).—b. 1897; ed. Rugby Sch.; solr. (sup. ct., Eng.); mil. serv., 1915-19 and 1941, lt.-col.; cadet, Ken., 1925; prov. comsnr., 1945; mil. gov., Som., 1948; gov. and c.-in-c., Som., 1948.
REED, C. G.—b. 1906; ed. Christ's Hosp. and King's Coll., Lond.; mil. serv., 1941-46, maj.; divisional educ. offr., Surrey, 1946; dir. educ., Barb., 1947.

REVE, W. H.—b. 1906; ed. Ossett Grammar Sch. and Univ. of Leeds; mil. serv., 1940-43, capt.; asst. field geol., Tang., 1935; Nyasa, 1943; engnr.-geol., P.W.D., Ken., 1943; sen. engnr.-geol., 1949; ch. geol., dept. lab. and mines, N. RENNER, E. A., O.B.E. (1948).—b. 1894; ed. Wesleyan Boys' High Sch., S.L., Parkfield Sch., Liverpool, and Edin. Univ.; med. offr., S.L., 1920; pathol., 1934; S.M.O., 1940; asst. D.M.S., 1948; D.D.M.S., 1949; D.M.S., 1952.

RENNIE, A. B.—b. 1896; ed. Wolmer's Sch., J'ca, King's Coll., Lond., and Gray's Inn; barrister-at-law; mil. serv., 1916-19, lieut.; elk. of cts. and ag. res. mag., J'ca, 1929; res. mag., 1933; cr. solr., 1934; puisne judge, 1949; proper offr. of the crown.

WORMAL, W. G.—b. 1906; ed. Lincoln Sch. and Peterhouse, Camb.; mil. serv., 1940-46, lt.-col.; cadet, Nig., 1928; admin. offr., cl. IV, 1931; cl. III, 1938; seccon. labour offr., 1940-44; stats. offr., H.K., 1945; supplies offr., H.K. plan unit, C.O., 1945; gov. statistician, H.K., 1948; cl. II, M.C.S., and prin. asst. sec., offr. of comsnr.-gen., S.E. Asia, 1951; H.K. rep. on F.E. econ. and supplies comtce., C.O., 1945; H.K. del., S.E. Asia reg. conf. on stats., 1948; comsnr. of regisn., 1949; H.K. rep. conf. of col. govt. statisticians, Lond., 1950.
WORRELL, C. T. W. E.—b. 1894; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trin.; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; mag., Trin., 1933; asst. law offr., later first cr. coun., 1936; solr. gen., 1951.

WRAY, M. O.—b. 1912; ed. St. George's Sch., Harpenden and Wadham Coll., Oxford; admin. offr., Uga., 1935; admin. sec., Zanz., 1949; asst. ch. sec. to the high comsnr. for Basuto., Bech. Prot. and Swaz., 1952.
WREFORD, C. K.—b. 1906; ed. Cranleigh and Clare Coll., Camb.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; cadet, Nig., 1928; admin. offr., cl. III, 1931; cl. II, 1946; cl. I, 1949.

WRIGHT, Sir Andrew, K.C.M.G. (1948), C.M.G. (1941), C.B.E. (1932), M.C. (bar).—b. 1895; ed. Haileybury and Jesus Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1914-19, maj., R. of O., 1940-42, lt.-col., M.E. Forces, assst. sec., Cyp., 1922; ch. assst. sec., 1930; col. sec., 1937; ag. gov., 1938-39; col. sec., Trin., 1943; ag. gov., 1943-46; gov. and c-in-c., Gam., 1947; Cyp., 1949.

WRIGHT, J.—b. 1904; ed. Dunfermline High Sch., St. Andrews and Edin. Univs., East of Scotland Coll. of Agric. and I.C.T.A.; mil. serv., 1939-42, capt.; mycologist, G.C., 1929; inspr., plants and produce, 1931; asst. supt., agric., 1933; supt., 1936; senr. agric. offr., 1945; J'ca, 1946; dep. dir., agric. (research), 1949; dir., agric., 1951; served on war emerg. comtees., Ashanti, 1942-45; author of papers on botany, plant path. entom., and gen. agric. in sci. journals.

WRIGHT, R. A.—b. 1912; ed. Roxborough, Eastbourne and R.V. Coll., Lond.; mil. serv., 1944-46; vet. offr., Mal, S.S., 1937; attd. commonwealth health dept., Canberra, 1942; vet. offr., Ken. (secondment), 1943; dep. food contrlr., S'pore, 1945; state vet. offr., Johore, Mal., 1946; ch. vet. offr., S'pore, 1950.

WYLIE, C., Q.C., E.D.—b. 1905; ed. Auckland Gram. Sch. and N.Z. Univ.; barrister, Inner Temple; mil. serv., 1941-46, maj. (desps.) ; cr. coun., Mal., 1946; senr. fedl. coun., 1950; atty.-gen., Barb., 1951.

WYN-HARRIS, Sir Percy, K.C.M.G. (1950), M.B.E.—b. 1903; ed. Gresham's Sch., Holt, and Caius Coll., Camb.; cadet, Ken., 1926; prov. comsnr., 1945; ch. native comsnr., 1947; gov. and c-in-c., Gam., 1949; settlement offr. (Kikuyu land claims), 1939-40; labour liaison offr., 1944.
YARNOLD, D. H.—b. 1908; ed. Cheltenham Gram. Sch.; mil. serv., 1939-46; exec. engr., P.W.D., Nig., 1937; D.D.P.W., Gam., 1951.

YEARSLEY, V. C. R., O.B.E. (1953).—b. 1891; ed. High Sch., Newcastle-u-Lyme; ent. C.A. off. after compet. exam., 1912; mil. serv., 1914-19; asst. hd., stores dept., 1928; shipping dept., 1931; stores dept., 1934; dep. hd., stores dept., 1938; hd. stores dept., 1945; engnr. stores dept., 1949; appts. and passages dept., 1952.

YEO, Kok Cheang.—b. 1903; ed. Hong Kong Univ. and Camb. Univ.; lect. and examiner in public health, H.K. Univ., 1936-37; asst. M.O.H., H.K., 1928; Chinese health offr., senr. gr., 1939; dep. dir., health serv., 1947; dep. dir., med. and health serv., 1950; dir., 1952.

YORK, A. D.—b. 1907; interned, 1942-45; cadet, M.C.S., 1929; cl. V, 1932; cl. IV., 1936; cl. III, 1941; cl. II, 1946; cl. IB, 1948; p.a.s., comsnr.-gen., S.E. Asia, 1950; ag. sec. for def., S'pore, 1952; ag. dep. ch. sec., Mal., 1952.

YOUNG, A. H., C.B.E.—b. 1899; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edin., and Edin. Univ.; mil. serv., 1917-19, 2nd lieut.; inspr., produce, Nig., 1926; dep. suptg. inspr., 1927; suptg. inspr., 1928; dep. dir. of commerce and industry, 1945; dir., marketing and exports dept., 1948; chmn., Cameroons Dev't. Corpn., 1952.
YOUNG, G. L., O.B.E. (1950).—b. 1903; ed. Sandys Gram. Sch. and Lyceum Sch., Berm.; clk., Berm., 1917; asst. col. treas., 1930; audr., 1952.

YOUNG, R. M.—b. 1900; ed. Mountjoy Sch. and Dublin Univ.; p.o.w., 1942-45; European mstr., S.S., 1924; headmstr., 1929; supervsr., private schs., S'pore, 1938; prin., Anderson Sch., Ipoh, 1946; dep. dir., educ., S'pore, 1946; ag. dir., 1952.

YOUNG, W. M.—b. 1917; ed. Wellington Coll. and Camb. Univ.; interned, 1942-45; cadet, M.C.S., 1939; cl. V, 1942 (retros.); cl. IV, 1946; asst. comsnr., labour, 1946; cl. III, 1950; ag. dep. comsnr., labour, Mal., 1952.

ZANNETIDES, C., Licencié en Droit.—b. 1898; ed. Académie d'Aix, Univ. d'Aix, Marseilles; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; off. receiver in bankruptcy, 1940-41; dist. judge, Cyp., 1941; pres., dist. ct., 1949.

ZEKIA, M.—b. 1903; ed. Univ. of Istanbul; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; dist. judge, Cyp., 1940; pres., dist. ct., 1947; puisne judge, 1952; comsnr. of enq. for Lefkonico incident, 1945; chmn., comtce. on Turkish affrs., 1948.
ZENON, L. E.—b. 1903; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; Licencié en Droit, Univ. of Paris; dist. judge, Cyp., 1942; pres., dist. ct., 1952.
